nodes.title: Adv Medical Microbiology w/lab
nodes.description: Comprehensive study of human pathogenic bacteria and their disease states in humans. Laboratory sessions provide practical experience in handling and identifying these pathogens. Credit not awarded for both MMG 3220 and MMG 5220. Prerequisite: MMG 2010.
nodes.other_requirements: MLS, MGEN and MICR majors only; Must enroll in lab MMG 3220 L01; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for MMG 3220 ZRA

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry 1
nodes.description: Properties and reactivity of organic compounds with consideration of bonding, stereochemistry, and reaction mechanisms. For premedical and biological sciences students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 2580 and CHEM 1580, CHEM 1582, or CHEM 1500. Prerequisite: CHEM 1450 or CHEM 1455.
nodes.other_requirements: $85 fee for lab supplies; Must register for a CHEM 2580 lab L01-L23; Degree students only; Post Bac Pre Med students register for CHEM 2580 ZRA-ZRC

nodes.title: Hematology
nodes.description: Advanced theory and analysis of blood cell physiology and related pathology. Concepts of hemostasis and clinical assessment methods. Credit not awarded for both MLS 3200 and MLS 5200. Prerequisites: One semester of organic chemistry, one semester of biochemistry.
nodes.other_requirements: One semester of organic chemistry. MLS, BMCS, MLS PBC PACE students, or Instructor Permission. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Fndtn of Interprofessionalism
nodes.description: Ensures that Medical Laboratory Science students are adequately prepared for beginning their clinical practica. During this online course, students will develop interprofessional communication skills, explore team dynamics, reflect on professional behaviors and their values, discuss ethics within the field of laboratory sciences, and review the goals, expectations, and responsibilities of a student learner in a professional laboratory environment. Prerequisite: 4th year Medical Laboratory Science student.
nodes.other_requirements: 4th year Medical Laboratory Science student. MLS Seniors and MLS PBC Students only.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Clin Practicum:Microbiology
nodes.description: Experiences in an approved clinical laboratory education site in the area of clinical microbiology. Prerequisite: Medical Laboratory Science Seniors only.
nodes.other_requirements: Medical Laboratory Science Seniors only. MLS Seniors and MLS PBC students only.

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 2
nodes.description: Part II of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body. Prerequisite: ANPS 1190.
nodes.other_requirements: Common hour exams are held 5:30-7:00pm on these Mondays: 2/9, 3/2, and 4/6. BIOM,CSD,DIET,DNFS,EXSC,HSCI,MLS,NFS, PE, PHS, MRS, PRNU; Majors only until Dec. 2nd, Please contact Patrick Mullen (patrick.c.mullen@uvm.edu) with any questions; Class meets 3x a week with online lab comprising 4th credit; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for ANPS 1200 ZRA; Prereq enforced by the system:ANPS 1190.

nodes.title: General Chemistry 2
nodes.description: Second semester of a two-semester sequence. Topics include solutions, kinetics, equilibrium, acid-base chemistry, aqueous ionic equilibria, thermodynamics, electrochemistry, and nuclear chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1450 and CHEM 1455. Prerequisite: CHEM 1400 or CHEM 1405.
nodes.other_requirements: $60 fee for lab supplies; Degree students only;

nodes.title: Intro to Forensic Diagnostics
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Students will need to complete laboratory safety requirements during the first week of class and wear appropriate clothing to class each week as it will be held in the laboratory.

nodes.title: Intro to Human Disease
nodes.description: Elementary course in human pathology designed for Allied Health students. First portion deals with general mechanisms of disease, followed by disorders of specific organs. Prerequisites: ANPS 1190 and ANPS 1200.
nodes.other_requirements: MLS, MRS, NMT and RADT majors only; Prerequisites: College biology and two successfully completed semesters of Anatomy and Physiology.

nodes.title: Clinical Practicum: Chemistry
nodes.description: Experiences in an approved clinical laboratory education site in the area of clinical chemistry. Prerequisite: Medical Laboratory Science Seniors only.
nodes.other_requirements: Medical Laboratory Science Seniors only. MLS Seniors and MLS PBC students only.

nodes.title: Advanced Immunobiology
nodes.description: Advanced survey of key current topics in immunology. Focus on understanding the key concepts and experimental approaches in the major areas in immunology, with an emphasis on applications to human disease. Prerequisites: Cell Biology and Biochemistry recommended.
nodes.other_requirements: recommended

nodes.title: Clinical Practicum:Hematology
nodes.description: Experiences in approved clinical laboratory education site in the area of clinical hematology. Prerequisite: Medical Laboratory Science Seniors only.
nodes.other_requirements: Medical Laboratory Science Seniors only. MLS Seniors and MLS PBC students only; Class time will rotate with MLS 3192,3392, and 3492; Combined time commitment is M-F 8:00 - 5:00; Offsite Clinical Affiliates; Course Fee $60.00

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Blood Bank
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Clinical Chemistry I
nodes.description: Lectures and laboratory experiences introduce basic principles in clinical quantitative analysis and laboratory instrumentation; test results are correlated with clinical case studies. Credit not awarded for both MLS 3100 and MLS 5100. Prerequisites: ANPS 1190 and ANPS 1200; CHEM 1400 and CHEM 1450; CHEM 1580 or CHEM 2580.

nodes.title: Research Capstone
nodes.description: The third course in a three-course research series. Complete the capstone project under the guidance of the research mentor or the Graduate Program Director/Course Instructor. Communicate the findings both through a formal oral presentation and a written research paper. Prerequisites: NH 6899, MLS 6400, MLS 6500.
nodes.other_requirements: Contact instructor for meeting location and time

nodes.title: Clinical Chemistry II
nodes.description: Advanced instruction in body chemistry and pathophysiology of disease with emphasis on diagnostic lab techniques in chemistry. Prerequisites: MLS 3100, PATH 2010.
nodes.other_requirements: UG MLS, and MLS PBC students only

nodes.title: Immunohematology
nodes.description: Advanced theory and experience related to human blood groups and transfusion practice. Credit not awarded for both MLS 3400 and MLS 5400. Prerequisite: BHSC 3420 or MMG 3230.
nodes.other_requirements: MLS, BMCS, MLS PBC PACE students: Prereqs enforced by the system: Immunohematology Lab Fee: $70.00; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Microbiol & Infectious Disease
nodes.description: An introduction to basic microbiology and microbes that cause infectious diseases, with a focus on microbial structure, function, metabolism, ecology, and pathogenesis. Prerequisites: BHSC 1340, BCOR 1400, BCOR 1425, BIOL 1005 or BIOL 1400; Microbiology or Molecular Genetics major or minor or Animal Sciences, Biomedical and Clinical Sciences major.
nodes.other_requirements: Microbiology or Molecular Genetics major or minor or Animal Sciences, Biomedical and Clinical Sciences major. Minimum sophomore standing; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE and Post Bac Pre Med students register for MMG 2010 ZRA; Eligible majors: ASCI, BISC, ENSC, MGEN, MICR, MLS, PBIO, HSCI, PHS,BMCS majors and MICR minors only until 5/01/2026.

nodes.title: Hematology Lab
nodes.description: The laboratory sessions will cover four broad areas of hematology practice: 1) laboratory tests associated with hemostasis, 2) laboratory tests and morphology seen in diseases affecting WBC cells, 3) peripheral blood and bone marrow cell morphology, and 4) laboratory tests and the associated morphology diagnostic of various RBC disorders. Prerequisite: One semester of organic chemistry, one semester of biochemistry. Co-requisite: MLS 3200.
nodes.other_requirements: MLS, BMCS or MLS PBC PACE Students; Or Instructor Permission; $70 lab fee; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 1
nodes.description: Part I of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereg limited to: BMCS, BIOM, CSD, EMS, EXSC, HSCI, MLS, MRS, NFS, NMT, PE, PHS, PRNU majors only. Degree students only even after major restrictions lifted.

nodes.title: General Chemistry 1
nodes.description: First semester of a two-semester sequence. Topics include matter, stoichiometry, gas laws, thermochemistry, quantum theory, atomic structure, electronic configurations, bonding, and intermolecular forces. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1400 and CHEM 1100, CHEM 1102, or CHEM 1405.

nodes.title: Applied Medical Diagnostics
nodes.description: Introduces the methodologies, techniques, and clinical applications associated with point of care testing in a community health setting. Students explore community health issues and engage in service-learning hours with a community partner outside of scheduled class time. Service learning combines community service with academic instruction, focusing on critical, reflective thinking. Prerequisites: MLS 2010 or a Medical Laboratory Science major; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing

nodes.title: Clinical Practicum
nodes.description: Clinical Practicum involves a semester long directed clinical practice in Hematology, Chemistry, Microbiology, Immunohematology, and Molecular Biology at assigned clinical affiliate sites. Prerequisites: MLS 3300, MMG 3230, MLS 3100, MMG 3220, MLS 3110, MLS 3200, MLS 3400.
nodes.other_requirements: MLSC Grad students only; Offsite Clinical Affiliate 8-5

nodes.title: Clinical Microbiology II
nodes.description: Comprehensive study of non-bacterial pathogenic microorganisms and their disease states in humans. Includes medical mycology, parasitology and virology. Credit not awarded for both MLS 3300 and MLS 5300. Prerequisites: MMG 1650 or MMG 2010.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both MLS 3300 and MLS 5300. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: MLS Certification Review
nodes.description: Seminar on topics in the practice and profession of Medical Laboratory Science. Online course. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Co-requisites: MLS 3192, MLS 3292, MLS 3392, MLS 3492, Medical Laboratory major.
nodes.other_requirements: MLS 3192 , MLS 3292 , MLS 3392 , MLS 3492 , Medical Laboratory major. MLS Seniors & MLS PBC students only; Lab Fee: $60.00;

nodes.title: Clin Practicum:Immunohematolog
nodes.description: Experiences in an approved clinical laboratory education site in the area of clinical immunohematology. Prerequisite: Medical Laboratory Science Seniors only.
nodes.other_requirements: Medical Laboratory Science Seniors only. MLS Seniors and MLS PBC students only.

nodes.title: Intro Organic Chemistry w/lab
nodes.description: Properties and reactivity of basic organic compounds of technological and biological significance. Not recommended for pre-medical students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1580 and CHEM 1150, CHEM 1152, CHEM 1582, CHEM 1500, or CHEM 2580. Prerequisite: CHEM 1100 or CHEM 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: Not recommended for pre-medical students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1580 and  CHEM 1150 ,  CHEM 1152 ,  CHEM 1582 ,  CHEM 1500 , or  CHEM 2580 . Must register for a CHEM 1580 lab, L01-L15; $85 fee: Chem Lab Supplies; Cross listed withCHEM 1582A. PACE students with permission and override. All labs must be completed in order to pass the course. Attendance for all lectures and exams is expected. All work must be completed by the due date. The final exam must be taken to pass the course.

nodes.title: Immunology
nodes.description: Deals with cells, organs, development, interactions and the functioning (infectious process, immunodeficiency, hypersensitivity reactions, transplantation and tumor immunology) of the innate and the adaptive immune system. Prerequisites: One semester of cell biology or similar course is encouraged.
nodes.other_requirements: One semester of cell biology or similar course is encouraged. Degree students only, PACE students register for BHSC 3420 ZRA

nodes.title: Microbiology & Pathogenesis
nodes.description: Overview of microbiology, emphasizing the relationships between the structure, metabolism, and genetics of microorganisms and their roles in nature and in pathogenesis. Prerequisite: One semester chemistry. Not intended for students who have completed BIOL 1400 and BIOL 1450 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: One semester chemistry. Not intended for students who have completed BIOL 1400 and BIOL 1450 or equivalent. Students with BIOL 1400 and 1450 or BCOR 1400 and 1450 should register for MMG 2010; 4 Hrs Chemistry; Must register for an MMG 1650 lab; Additional Lab fee: $50.00; PRNU majors only, or by instructor permission; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for MMG 1650 ZRA; Colocated with MMG 1650 ZRA;

nodes.title: Certification Review
nodes.description: Certification review of the Medical Laboratory Science Body of Knowledge. It is designed to provide a challenging self directed assessment of practical and theoretical knowledge and will prepare students to successfully pass the ASCP certification exam in Medical Laboratory Science. Prerequisites: MLS 3300, MMG 3230, MLS 3100, MMG 3220, MLS 3110, MLS 3200, MLS 3400. Pre/Co-requisite: MLS 6700.
nodes.other_requirements: Pre/Co-requisite: MLS 6700. MLSC Grad students only; Student fee: $60

nodes.title: Fundamentals Critical Inquiry
nodes.description: Interprofessional research/evidence-based practice course; instructs students from a variety of healthcare professions to develop skills in research/clinical question development, appraisal of literature and practice guidelines, experimental, quantitative and qualitative studies, and a fundamental understanding of evidence to inform their practice.
